articles.summary: If you want to keep up to date with the latest innovations in developer infrastructure [4], you should save your seat for Redis Released: Worldwide [4] (virtual).

articles.title: See the full agenda and join live from anywhere >>
THE TRUE NATURE OF USEACTIONSTATE
articles.summary: The useActionState hook in React 19 helps manage return values from server functions and makes the user experience for asynchronous operations. This article introduces a simple TODO app built with useReducer and shows how to refactor it to utilize useActionState for persistence, adding optimistic UI updates and error handling. Integrating useActionState with useOptimistic makes the user experience much better and smoother.

articles.title: WHAT HAPPENS WHEN YOU MAKE A MOVE IN LICHESS.ORG?
YOU SHOULD PROBABLY PAY ATTENTION TO TOKENIZERS
articles.summary: Tokenizers break down text into smaller units. Their ability to handle different types of data, such as emojis, misspelled words, and dates, can affect the accuracy of RAG models. Poor tokenization can lead to incorrect embeddings and flawed results from LLMs. Data should be carefully preprocessed and standardized for better LLM retrieval reliability.

articles.title: WHY I'M LEAVING OPENAI AND WHAT I'M DOING NEXT
articles.summary: Miles Brundage, former Senior Advisor for AGI Readiness at OpenAI, is leaving the company to focus on AI policy research and advocacy from a more independent position. He believes that while OpenAI is making progress towards AI safety, both the organization and the world at large are not yet ready for the potential of artificial general intelligence (AGI).

articles.title: A LOVE LETTER TO GOLANG
articles.summary: Go is a valuable programming language for building web applications thanks to its streamlined syntax, robust standard library, and concurrency features. Go frameworks like Templ make full-stack development much easier.

articles.title: OOP IS NOT THAT BAD, ACTUALLY
articles.summary: Mainstream statically-typed OOP enables straightforward and backwards-compatible evolution of types, making them easy to compose. This post shows a simple example involving a logging library and a database library to demonstrate how OOP allows for adding new functionality and dependencies without breaking existing code.

articles.title: WORKSHOP: CLEAN ARCHITECTURE IN NEXT.JS
articles.summary: * What is Clean Architecture? * What problems does it solve? * How do you implement Clean Architecture in Next.js [11]?

articles.title: TOOLPAD
articles.summary: Toolpad is a suite of React components and a low-code builder designed for rapidly developing dashboards and internal tools. It consists of Toolpad Core, a set of React components, and Toolpad Studio, a self-hosted low-code admin builder.

articles.title: OPENAI-REALTIME-API
articles.summary: Openai-realtime-api is a TypeScript client for OpenAI's real-time voice API that provides a fully typed and bug-fixed alternative to the official JavaScript version. It has 100% compatibility with OpenAI's events, supports various runtimes like Node.js, browser, and Deno, and includes Node.js CLI examples for testing.

articles.title: REACT NATIVE BOTTOM TABS
articles.summary: React Native Bottom Tabs uses native platform components to implement bottom navigation in React Native apps.

articles.title: HOW MICROSOFT EDGE IS REPLACING REACT WITH WEB COMPONENTS
articles.summary: Microsoft's Edge browser team is transitioning from React to native web components with the WebUI 2.0 project to improve performance, especially on low-end devices. The team aims to convert about 50% of existing React-based UIs to web components by year-end, focusing on a "markup-first architecture.”

articles.title: ASYNC RUST IN THREE PARTS
articles.summary: Async/await in Rust is a way to achieve concurrency without using threads. Threads, while efficient for small numbers of tasks, become inefficient and resource-intensive when dealing with thousands of tasks. Async/await, using futures and tasks, is a more scalable and efficient way to manage concurrency, especially for tasks that involve I/O operations.

articles.title: STATE OF REACT 2024 SURVEY
articles.summary: The State of React 2024 Survey aims to measure the awareness and popularity of React APIs and libraries in the React ecosystem.

articles.title: AWS DATA CENTER LATENCIES VISUALIZED
articles.summary: A world map visualization of AWS data center latencies by milliseconds.

articles.title: BUILDING FOCUS LIKE A MUSCLE
articles.summary: Improving focus is like building muscle — start with manageable focus periods and gradually increase them by using timers and breaking tasks into small parts.

articles.title: I GOT PROMOTED IN < 1.5 YEARS AT AMAZON TO MID LEVEL BECAUSE…
articles.summary: This developer was promoted at Amazon quickly after successfully completing a complex migration project thanks to techniques like gating mechanisms, which helped make sure it happened smoothly.

articles.title: EVERYTHING I BUILT WITH CLAUDE ARTIFACTS THIS WEEK
articles.summary: This developer built 14 interactive projects in a week using Claude Artifacts, finding Claude very versatile for both rapid prototyping and solving immediate problems quickly.
